summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the sermon by reading 2 Corinthians 5:5, which speaks of God giving us a taste of heaven in our hearts so we won't settle for less. He titled the message 'Nothing Less Than God's Best,' emphasizing that God has a specific, prosperous, and hopeful plan for each person, as declared in Jeremiah 29:11. Using the story of Abraham and Sarah from Genesis, Pastor Travis illustrated how God often gives 'preposterous' or seemingly impossible promises that require patient faith. He acknowledged that waiting for these promises to be fulfilled can be incredibly difficult, leading to temptation. The core of the message focused on what to do in the 'pass through'—the waiting period between receiving a promise and seeing it fulfilled. Pastor Travis provided three crucial directives: don't settle for less than God's best, don't compromise your convictions or God's standard, and don't lose hope. He warned that settling or compromising, as Abraham and Sarah did with Hagar, has long-term consequences and costs not only the individual but also those around them. Conversely, waiting faithfully, though challenging, leads to a sweet reward. He closed by inviting those who had never given their lives to Jesus to do so, emphasizing God's forgiving and welcoming nature.
mainPoints: Do not settle for anything less than God's best plan and promise for your life, even when the wait is long and difficult.
Do not compromise your convictions or God's standard by trying to fulfill His promises through your own impatience and methods.
Do not lose hope in the waiting period; trust that God is on time and the fulfillment of a longing is sweet to the soul.

versesMentioned.text: Now the one who has fashioned us for this very purpose is God, who has given us the Spirit as a deposit, guaranteeing what is to come.
For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the Lord, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.
Abraham fell facedown; he laughed and said to himself, “Will a son be born to a man a hundred years old? Will Sarah bear a child at the age of ninety?”
Is anything too hard for the Lord? I will return to you at the appointed time next year, and Sarah will have a son.”
Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.

When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flames will not set you ablaze.
For the revelation awaits an appointed time; it speaks of the end and will not prove false. Though it linger, wait for it; it will certainly come and will not delay.
A longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul, but fools detest turning from evil.

Monday.scripture: Jeremiah 29:11 - "For I know the plans I have for you," declares the Lord, "plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future."
Monday.focus: God's specific and good plan for you.
Monday.action: Write down one 'preposterous' promise or dream you believe God has placed in your heart.
Monday.prayer: God, thank you that your plans for me are good. Help me to trust your specific purpose for my life today.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 19:26 - "With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible."
Tuesday.focus: God's power to do the impossible.
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where you've been tempted to think "this is impossible" and consciously surrender it to God in prayer.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, I bring my impossible situation to you. I choose to believe that with you, all things are possible. Strengthen my faith.

Wednesday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 - "Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up."
Wednesday.action: Perform one act of kindness or 'well-doing' today without expecting an immediate return.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, when I grow weary, remind me of the harvest to come. Renew my strength to persist in doing good.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 43:2 - "When you pass through the waters, I will be with you..."
Thursday.focus: God's presence in the difficult 'pass through' moments.
Thursday.action: Reflect on a current challenge. Acknowledge God's presence with you in it by writing down, "God is with me in this."
Thursday.prayer: God, thank you for your promise to be with me in every trial. Help me to feel your presence and find power in this pass through.

Friday.scripture: Habakkuk 2:3 - "For the revelation awaits an appointed time... it will certainly come and will not delay."
Friday.action: Choose to postpone a decision you feel rushed to make, committing to pray about it for one more day.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I trust your appointed time. Guard my heart from impatience and help me to wait faithfully for your promises.

Saturday.scripture: Proverbs 13:19 - "A longing fulfilled is sweet to the soul..."
Saturday.action: Encourage someone else who is in a season of waiting. Share a scripture or a word of hope with them.
Saturday.prayer: God, make my hope steadfast. Let the anticipation of your fulfillment be sweet, and use me to bring hope to others.
